summary.insufficient: Insufficient validation data. Complete research, VOC, and evidence collection to unlock AI recommendations.
summary.goStrong: Strong GO signal. Market, evidence, and customer data support proceeding with confidence.
summary.go: GO recommendation. Core validation criteria are met with manageable risk.
summary.review: Conditional GO — proceed with targeted improvements in weak score categories.
summary.hold: HOLD recommendation. Critical validation gaps require resolution before investment.
summary.needVoc: Customer evidence is insufficient. VOC collection should be the immediate priority.
summary.needGrants: Government grant mapping is incomplete. Explore funding programs to de-risk capital needs.
summary.needCompetitors: Competitive landscape is under-mapped. Add competitors before final GO decision.

summary.moderate: PRD in draft. Complete before development planning.

summary.strong: Development specs are ready for engineering handoff.

summary.strong: Knowledge base is ready for AI-powered queries.

insights.ready: Knowledge chunks available for AI queries
insights.process: Process evidence to build knowledge base

insights.ready: Document ready for review and export

summary.strong: Document portfolio is investor-ready. Review, export, and share with stakeholders.
summary.moderate: Core documents exist. Complete remaining AI Studio outputs for a full package.
summary.weak: No AI documents generated yet. Start with the Validation Report.

expert.insufficient: Insufficient data for a confident opinion. Complete research and validation first.

vc.label: VC Opinion
vc.positive: Market size and business model show investable signals. Due diligence on unit economics recommended.
vc.neutral: Market opportunity is plausible but requires stronger traction and clearer moat before Series A.
vc.negative: Market validation is weak. Revisit TAM analysis and revenue model before investor conversations.

pm.label: PM Opinion
pm.positive: Problem-solution fit is well supported by VOC data. MVP scope can be narrowed confidently.
pm.neutral: Customer pain is documented but sample size is limited. Run more structured interviews.
pm.negative: Problem hypothesis lacks customer evidence. Prioritize discovery before building features.

cto.label: CTO Opinion
cto.positive: Technical execution path is feasible with current evidence base. Architecture decisions can proceed.
cto.neutral: Core technical risks are identified but need deeper validation on scalability and integrations.
cto.negative: Execution feasibility is unclear. Add technical evidence and define MVP architecture.

marketing.label: Marketing Opinion
marketing.positive: Competitive positioning is differentiated. GTM narrative can be sharpened for early adopters.
marketing.neutral: Competitive landscape is mapped but differentiation message needs refinement.
marketing.negative: Market positioning is undifferentiated. Complete competitor analysis and value proposition work.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.desc: Everything you need to know about LaunchLens.

q1.question: What is an AI Strategy Consultant?
q1.answer: LaunchLens is an AI-powered strategy platform that automates market research, competitive analysis, evidence collection, and executive reporting — guiding you to a defensible GO / NO GO decision.

q2.question: How does market research work?
q2.answer: The AI orchestrator runs structured research tasks across market sizing, trends, and customer segments. Results feed into the Evidence library with confidence scores.

q3.question: Is SWOT generated automatically?
q3.answer: Yes. The Framework Engine applies SWOT, Porter's Five Forces, and other consulting frameworks based on your project data and market analysis.

q4.question: Does it analyze government grant programs?
q4.answer: Yes. Government module maps K-Startup, ministry programs, and grant fit scores to your project profile.

q5.question: Can I export reports as PPT?
q5.answer: Executive Report supports PDF, PPT, and DOCX export through the report preview and export queue.

q6.question: Do I need to log in?
q6.answer: Sign in with Google to save your projects. Use 'Try live demo' to explore without an account.

q7.question: How is GO / NO GO calculated?
q7.answer: The Decision Engine scores research, evidence, VOC, competitors, and grants — with explainable drivers and missing-data checklist.

q8.question: Is my data used to train AI models?
q8.answer: Your project data stays in your workspace. Real LLM integration (Sprint 9.0+) will follow enterprise privacy standards.

q9.question: Who is LaunchLens for?
q9.answer: Startups, corporate strategy teams, consultants, VCs, and public-sector innovators validating new initiatives.

q10.question: When will paid plans launch?
q10.answer: Pro and Enterprise plans are coming soon. The Free plan includes the full workspace available today.

q11.question: What is Private Beta?
q11.answer: Private Beta gives you unlimited access to every LaunchLens feature at no cost while we refine the product with early adopters. Your feedback shapes the roadmap.

footer.ctaTitle: Ready to validate your next big bet?
footer.ctaDesc: Start free with Google — no credit card required.

legal.privacyBody: LaunchLens respects your data. Project information is stored in your workspace and is not sold to third parties. Full privacy policy will be published before Sprint L2 authentication launch.
legal.termsBody: By using LaunchLens you agree to use the platform for lawful business strategy purposes. Full terms of service will be published before paid plans launch.

decision.emptyDescription: Create a project to generate an AI strategic decision.
decision.emptyInsufficientDescription: Inputs for {project} are still thin. Add research and evidence so AI can suggest GO/HOLD/NO GO.

executive.insufficient1: Insufficient data to render a definitive strategic recommendation.
executive.insufficient2: Complete research, evidence, and customer validation inputs before proceeding to investment decisions.
executive.insufficient3: Current posture: HOLD pending evidence completion.
executive.go2: Market attractiveness and evidence quality support a conditional GO recommendation.
executive.go3: Execution readiness is adequate; proceed with phased investment and milestone gates.
executive.noGo2: Competitive intensity and evidence gaps outweigh near-term market opportunity.
executive.noGo3: Recommend deferring capital allocation until validation thresholds are met.
executive.hold2: Strategic potential exists but critical evidence gaps remain unresolved.
executive.hold3: Address priority actions below to elevate decision confidence.

startup.go1: Market validation signals are favorable for a startup launch trajectory.
startup.hold1: Startup viability is plausible but requires additional customer and market proof.
startup.noGo1: Current startup hypothesis lacks sufficient validation for a GO recommendation.

businessStrategy.go1: Strategic alignment and market conditions support proceeding with the initiative.
businessStrategy.hold1: Business strategy requires further competitive and financial validation.
businessStrategy.noGo1: Strategic risk profile exceeds acceptable thresholds for immediate execution.

aiInitiative.go1: AI initiative demonstrates clear value hypothesis with supporting evidence.
aiInitiative.hold1: AI initiative shows promise; technical and organizational readiness need confirmation.
aiInitiative.noGo1: AI initiative ROI case is insufficiently substantiated at this stage.

dx.go1: Digital transformation scope aligns with operational readiness indicators.
dx.hold1: DX program requires stronger execution planning and stakeholder alignment.
dx.noGo1: Digital transformation readiness does not justify near-term investment.

newBusiness.go1: New business line exhibits favorable market entry conditions.
newBusiness.hold1: New business opportunity warrants further market sizing and competitive analysis.
newBusiness.noGo1: New business entry risk exceeds projected returns based on current evidence.

marketExpansion.go1: Target market expansion shows positive demand and competitive positioning.
marketExpansion.hold1: Market expansion thesis requires localized validation and regulatory review.
marketExpansion.noGo1: Expansion market entry barriers outweigh identified opportunities.

default.go1: Aggregate evidence supports a conditional GO recommendation.
default.hold1: Strategic potential exists; evidence completion is required before commitment.
default.noGo1: Evidence base does not support a GO recommendation at this time.

go.market: Decision score of {score} reflects favorable market and validation signals.
go.evidence: High-confidence evidence base supports strategic conviction.
go.execution: Execution readiness at {score}% indicates operational feasibility.

noGo.market: Market signals do not meet minimum viability thresholds.
noGo.competition: Competitive landscape presents material entry barriers.
noGo.evidence: Evidence quality is insufficient to support investment decisions.

hold.voc: Customer validation at {count} interviews — minimum 10 required for GO confidence.
hold.competition: Competitive intelligence requires completion before final verdict.
hold.data: Data completeness at {percent}% — below decision threshold.

risks.evidenceMitigation: Prioritize government, academic, and primary research sources.

explainMode.title: Decision Logic
explainMode.stepDataDetail: Overall evidence coverage is {percent}% — threshold for GO is 55%+.
explainMode.stepScoreDetail: Composite score of {score} from research, evidence, VOC, competition, and grants.
explainMode.stepConfidenceDetail: Confidence index at {confidence}% based on evidence quality and source trust.
explainMode.stepVerdictGo: Score and confidence thresholds met — conditional GO issued.
explainMode.stepVerdictNoGo: Score below viability threshold with sufficient data — NO GO issued.
explainMode.stepVerdictHold: Insufficient data or borderline scores — HOLD pending evidence completion.
explainMode.stepFrameworkDetail: {count} frameworks applied with aggregate decision impact of {impact} ({names}).
explainMode.frameworkSectionDesc: AI applied these strategy frameworks as analytical evidence — not as standalone reports.
explainMode.stepMarketDetail: Market score {score} with CAGR {growth}% — decision impact {impact} pts.
explainMode.marketSectionDesc: AI market analysis feeding strategy frameworks and decision scoring.

summaries.pestel: PESTEL macro scan for {project} scores {score}/100 from regulatory and market signals.
summaries.porter: Porter analysis for {project} scores {score}/100 with {evidence} evidence-backed competitive inputs.
summaries.threec: 3C analysis for {project} scores {score}/100 across company, customer, and competitor dimensions.
summaries.stp: STP segmentation for {project} scores {score}/100 based on {voc} customer voice entries.
summaries.bcg: BCG portfolio view for {project} scores {score}/100 from growth and share indicators.
summaries.ansoff: Ansoff growth matrix for {project} scores {score}/100 on expansion readiness.
summaries.valuechain: Value chain analysis for {project} scores {score}/100 on operational leverage points.
summaries.bmc: Business Model Canvas for {project} scores {score}/100 on revenue and cost structure fit.
summaries.leancanvas: Lean Canvas for {project} scores {score}/100 on problem-solution and channel fit.
summaries.jtbd: JTBD analysis for {project} scores {score}/100 from {voc} customer job signals.

evidence.swotResearch: Market research and competitive intelligence inputs
evidence.swotVoc: Customer interview and VOC validation data
evidence.porterVoc: Buyer power and switching cost indicators
evidence.threecResearch: Company capability and market positioning data
evidence.bcgResearch: Portfolio growth and market share data
evidence.ansoffResearch: Growth pathway and market entry research
evidence.valuechainVoc: Process efficiency and customer delivery feedback
evidence.bmcResearch: Revenue model and cost structure research
evidence.jtbdVoc: Customer job statements and pain points

swot.i1Text: Evidence quality supports identifiable competitive strengths in the current data set.
swot.i2Text: Competitive and validation gaps remain that could weaken strategic positioning.

pestel.i1Text: Regulatory and market trends appear favorable for near-term strategic moves.
pestel.i2Text: Policy or economic uncertainty may constrain execution timing.

porter.i1Text: Competitive structure suggests moderate entry barriers with differentiation opportunities.
porter.i2Text: Rival intensity or buyer power may compress margins without clear differentiation.

threec.i1Text: Company capabilities align with identified customer needs in target segments.
threec.i2Text: Competitor advantages or capability gaps require mitigation before scaling.

stp.i1Text: Target segment definition is supported by customer validation signals.
stp.i2Text: Segment overlap or weak differentiation may dilute go-to-market effectiveness.

bcg.i1Text: Portfolio indicators suggest investable growth opportunities.
bcg.i2Text: Low-share or declining positions may require divestment or pivot.

ansoff.i1Text: Market penetration or development pathways show viable expansion options.
ansoff.i2Text: Diversification or new market entry carries elevated execution risk.

valuechain.i1Text: Primary activities show efficiency or differentiation potential.
valuechain.i2Text: Support activities or supplier dependencies may erode margin.

bmc.i1Text: Revenue streams and value proposition show coherent business model alignment.
bmc.i2Text: Cost structure or channel assumptions need further validation.

leancanvas.i1Text: Problem and solution hypotheses are partially validated by customer data.
leancanvas.i2Text: Acquisition channels or revenue model require additional testing.

jtbd.i1Text: Customer job-to-be-done is identifiable from VOC and research inputs.
jtbd.i2Text: Desired outcomes are not yet fully validated across target segments.

swot.r1Text: Double down on validated strengths while addressing competitive blind spots.
swot.r2Text: Complete VOC and competitor analysis before major resource commitment.

pestel.r1Text: Track regulatory and economic indicators affecting market entry timing.
pestel.r2Text: Evaluate government program alignment as strategic tailwind.

porter.r1Text: Identify defensible positioning against established competitors.
porter.r2Text: Build switching costs or niche focus to mitigate price pressure.

threec.r1Text: Match company strengths to highest-value customer segments.
threec.r2Text: Complete structured competitor comparison before scaling.

stp.r1Text: Validate segment size and willingness-to-pay with primary research.
stp.r2Text: Articulate clear differentiation vs. nearest competitors.

bcg.r1Text: Allocate resources to high-growth, high-share opportunities.
bcg.r2Text: Evaluate exit or pivot for low-growth portfolio elements.

ansoff.r1Text: Focus on existing market depth before geographic or product expansion.
ansoff.r2Text: Use phased gates for new market or product diversification.

valuechain.r1Text: Identify cost or differentiation improvements in core operations.
valuechain.r2Text: Invest in technology and procurement efficiency.

bmc.r1Text: Test pricing and unit economics with target customers.
bmc.r2Text: Identify scalable vs. fixed cost components in the model.

leancanvas.r1Text: Run structured customer discovery to confirm core problem.
leancanvas.r2Text: Experiment with acquisition channels before scaling spend.

jtbd.r1Text: Document functional, emotional, and social jobs from VOC data.
jtbd.r2Text: Quantify how well the solution delivers desired customer outcomes.

welcome.title: LaunchLens AI Strategy Consultant
welcome.body:
Hello.

I'm your LaunchLens AI Strategy Consultant.

I'll analyze this project with you.

Let me ask a few quick questions first.

q1.title: What are you trying to validate?
q1.hint: Describe the core hypothesis you want to test.
q1.placeholder: e.g. Seniors want a trusted matching service for hobbies

q2.title: Who is your primary customer?
q2.hint: Be specific about segment and context.
q2.placeholder: e.g. Active seniors aged 60–75 in urban Korea

q3.title: Who are your known competitors?
q3.hint: Direct or indirect — list what you know today.
q3.placeholder: e.g. Senior community apps, offline centers

q4.title: What worries you most right now?

onboardingConsultant.confirmClose: Leave the interview? Your progress will be lost.

summary.title: Here's what I understood
summary.desc: Confirm before I generate your research plan.

auth.loginDesc: Sign in with Google and start your first project in under 5 minutes.
auth.supabaseNotConfigured: Authentication is not configured. Contact support or try Demo mode.

wizard.title: Create your first project
wizard.desc: Answer a few questions — we'll set up your strategy workspace in about 3 minutes.

checklist.title: Today's tasks
checklist.desc: Complete these steps to generate your first report.

errors.tryAgain: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
errors.notFoundDescription: The page you requested does not exist or has been moved.
errors.maintenanceDescription: We are performing scheduled maintenance. Please check back shortly.
errors.unauthorizedDescription: You need to sign in to access this page.

pages.projectNotFoundDesc: It may have been deleted or the URL is incorrect.

proactive.tam: Last time, market size was insufficient. I recommend starting with TAM analysis today.
proactive.voc: Customer validation is still thin ({count} VOC entries). Let's deepen interviews first.
proactive.competitor: Competitor mapping is incomplete. Benchmark 2–3 direct players next.
proactive.evidence: Evidence is thin. Add high-confidence sources before Decision.
proactive.report: Decision is ready. Generate the executive report to close the loop.
proactive.continue: Welcome back — let's continue where we left off with AI research.

summary.default: Here's what matters for your project today.
summary.vocGap: VOC is still at {count} entries — prioritize customer interviews.
summary.competitorDone: Competitor research is updated. Review positioning next.
summary.grantUpdate: Government grant landscape changed — review fit scores.
summary.marketUpdate: Market growth signals updated — revisit TAM assumptions.
summary.decisionReady: Decision inputs are ready — review GO/HOLD/NO GO.

brief.aiSummary: AI summary compiled from project memory and recent activity.

why.research: Market validation needs more depth before Decision.
why.decision: Enough inputs exist — synthesize GO/HOLD/NO GO.
why.report: Decision is ready — export for stakeholders.

empty.hint: Start here — AI will build project memory as you research.

research.hint: Add a research plan and AI will structure market, competitor, and customer work. Link findings to evidence next.

evidence.hint: Three or more HIGH-confidence evidence items materially improve decision confidence.

competitor.hint: Map leader/challenger positions so competitive risk flows into your decision.

government.hint: Track grant programs so fit scores strengthen the investment case.

decision.hint: With enough research, evidence, and VOC, AI synthesizes GO/HOLD/NO GO with rationale.

report.hint: When the decision is ready, generate an executive report for stakeholders.

memory.hint: Research and decision activity builds AI memory of your project context.

notification.hint: AI notifies you when research completes, evidence is added, decisions are ready, or reports are generated.

panel.title: Notifications
panel.subtitle: AI watches markets, competitors, and grants — then tells you what matters.

inbox.empty: No notifications yet. Add watch items or complete research to get AI alerts.

watchList.empty: No items yet — add from suggestions below.

settings.desc: Choose which alerts AI should surface for this project.

brief.title: Today's AI briefing
brief.headline: Here's what changed while you were away

dashboard.title: AI notifications
dashboard.subtitle: AI surfaces what changed — you decide what to act on.

marketUpdate.title: Market data updated for {label}
marketUpdate.summary: Growth rate and TAM estimates were refreshed in your watch list.

marketGrowth.title: Market growth rate shifted
marketGrowth.summary: Healthcare AI segment shows accelerated funding this quarter.

competitorUpdate.title: {label} product update detected
competitorUpdate.summary: A competitor in your watch list released a new AI feature.

competitorLaunch.title: Competitor launched new AI feature
competitorLaunch.summary: OpenAI announced enterprise workflow tools relevant to your market.

governmentUpdate.title: {label} program update
governmentUpdate.summary: Deadline or eligibility criteria may have changed — review grants.

governmentDeadline.title: Government grant deadline approaching
governmentDeadline.summary: TIPS application window closes soon — check your eligibility.

evidence.title: Evidence is thin
evidence.summary: Add high-confidence sources before generating a defensible decision.

decision.title: Ready for decision
decision.summary: Enough inputs exist — synthesize GO/HOLD/NO GO now.

report.title: Report generation recommended
report.summary: Decision confidence is high — export for stakeholders.

inactive.title: 30+ days since last activity
inactive.summary: Your project context may be stale — review market and evidence.

researchComplete.title: Research complete
researchComplete.summary: Research plans are done — link findings to evidence next.

decisionReady.title: Decision ready
decisionReady.summary: Inputs are sufficient — review GO/HOLD/NO GO.

reportReady.title: Report ready
reportReady.summary: Generate an executive report for stakeholders.

marketResearch.title: Re-run market research
marketResearch.summary: AI recommends refreshing TAM and growth signals for your watch markets.

report.title: Generate executive report
report.summary: Decision confidence is strong — share a stakeholder-ready report.

evidence.title: Strengthen evidence base
evidence.summary: Coverage is below 70% — add sources before finalizing decision.

confidence.title: Decision confidence update
confidence.summary: Review drivers and missing data before your next stakeholder meeting.
